novel_features.name: Multi-city trip compare
novel_features.description: Compare 2+ cities side-by-side: dedicated-GF count, GF-menu count, total places, average rating, and top-rated picks per city — the question the consumer apps can't answer.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a cross-city local SQL join across cached city + business + review data. Find Me Gluten Free, Atly, and Gluten Dude all show one city at a time.
novel_features.example: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli trip compare lisbon porto madrid --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user is choosing between cities for a celiac trip; emits structured per-city counts an agent can rank or filter.

novel_features.name: City diff since last sync
novel_features.description: Show what changed in a city since the last time you synced it: added/removed places, rating drift, new low-star safety reports.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs the current city snapshot against the prior snapshot using sitemap <lastmod>. The consumer app re-renders from scratch every visit and never tells you what's new.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before a return visit to a city the user has tracked previously — surfaces only what's actually changed.

novel_features.name: Lat/lng radius search
novel_features.description: Find dedicated-GF or GF-menu places within a radius of a lat/lng using Haversine over locally cached schema.org geo data.
novel_features.rationale: The site is bucketed by city and ZIP, not by coordinates. Local geo math on parsed JSON-LD location.geo unlocks 'near here' on any seed point.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent has coordinates (a hotel, an attraction) and needs nearby celiac-safe options ranked.
novel_features.group: Geographic queries the site doesn't expose

novel_features.name: City stats rollup
novel_features.description: One-shot SQL aggregate over a city: count, average rating, p50/p90 rating, dedicated-GF percent, GF-menu percent. Filterable by cuisine.
novel_features.rationale: Find Me Gluten Free has no stats endpoint. The aggregate exists only because the local cache stores every biz with parsed rating + flags.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs a one-line answer to 'how celiac-friendly is city X for cuisine Y?' before drilling into specific places.

novel_features.name: City-wide recent reviews
novel_features.description: Newest reviews across every place in a city, sorted by datePublished. Filter to safety-report-style 1-2 star reviews with --max-rating.
novel_features.rationale: The site buries reviews per-business — there's no city-wide review feed. Cross-business review join is local-only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when checking for new safety reports across a city before a trip; the most recent low-stars are the highest-signal warnings.

novel_features.name: Watchlist with change report
novel_features.description: Watch specific places; periodic 'watch report' fetches each and reports rating drift and new low-rated reviews since the baseline.
novel_features.rationale: Per-business snapshot diff using sitemap <lastmod> + cached aggregateRating. Consumer apps have no watchlist-with-change-report.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this on a personal allowlist of trusted places; surfaces only places where something celiac-relevant changed.

novel_features.name: Cuisine cross-city compare
novel_features.description: For one cuisine across multiple cities: per-city count, dedicated-GF percent, top-rated place. Useful when planning where on a trip GF pizza (or burgers, or bakery) is most available.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the site's 214-filter taxonomy across cities; the site only renders one cuisine in one city per page.
novel_features.example: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli cuisines compare burgers us va richmond charlottesville --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when cuisine availability matters for the trip and the user wants to weigh cities by what's actually on offer.

novel_features.name: Safety-report review filter
novel_features.description: Filter cached reviews for one place to those mentioning got-glutened / cross-contamination keywords from a curated, deterministic list — no LLM.
novel_features.rationale: Find Me Gluten Free has no safety filter. Filter is keyword-only and lives in a static reference list, so it's deterministic and verifiable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before vetting a specific place; surfaces just the reviews that report a celiac reaction or cross-contamination.

narrative.headline: A celiac trip-planner's terminal companion: every Find Me Gluten Free city, cached locally with cross-city compare, change tracking, and review safety filters the consumer app doesn't have.
narrative.value_prop: The Find Me Gluten Free iOS/Android app is great for 'what's near me right now.' This CLI is for the celiac who plans a trip three weeks out, the parent who returns to the same six cities every year, and the AI agent helping a celiac client weigh dining options. Sync any city once, then run trip compare across cities, cities diff to see what changed since last visit, places near for lat/lng radius search, and reviews recent --max-rating 2 to surface fresh safety reports.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. The CLI reads only public city, business, and sitemap pages via standard HTTP. The site's ToS prohibits crawling and its robots.txt extra-disallows AI bots from /biz, /posts, /postal — so this CLI ships with an honest User-Agent identifying itself as a personal user tool, a 1 req/sec default rate limit (FMGF_RPS to override, capped at 5), and no bulk-export commands. Use it as a personal companion, not a crawler.

quickstart.command: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli cities sync us va richmond
quickstart.comment: Foundation: fetch every place in Richmond, parse schema.org JSON-LD, persist into the local SQLite cache so all later commands are offline-fast
City-wide aggregate (count, avg rating, p50/p90, dedicated %, GF-menu %) computed locally — Find Me Gluten Free has no equivalent endpoint

quickstart.command: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli trip compare richmond charlottesville norfolk --json
quickstart.comment: The differentiator: side-by-side per-city celiac-friendliness — this question simply isn't answerable in the consumer app
City-wide review feed sorted newest-first; --max-rating 2 surfaces the safety-report-style 1-2 star reviews most worth reading before a trip
After a second 'cities sync' run, this surfaces only what changed (added/removed places, count delta) — re-reading from scratch every visit is the consumer-app failure mode

troubleshoots.fix: Lower the rate via the global flag: --rate-limit 0.5 (one request every two seconds).
City has not been hydrated yet. Run: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli cities sync <country> <state> <city>
Haversine search runs over the local cache. Sync at least one city near the lat/lng first via 'cities sync', then retry.
Run 'cities sync <country> <state> <city>' a second time; diff compares the two most recent snapshots.

troubleshoots.symptom: places hydrate fails with 'no application/ld+json block found'
troubleshoots.fix: A small number of newly listed pages render without schema.org data. The biz exists; the rich detail will appear after the next site index pass. Re-try in 24 hours.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when planning a celiac-safe trip, weighing multiple cities, tracking changes to a personal allowlist, or feeding gluten-free restaurant data to an AI agent. It is structurally not the right tool for a celiac who only needs 'what's safe within a half mile of where I'm standing right now' — that's the consumer app's strength. Reach for this when the question is 'where on this trip is GF pizza most available?', 'what changed in Pittsburgh since I was last there?', or 'list every dedicated-GF place in Portland with at least a 4-star average and a recent review.'

recipes.title: Plan a 3-city trip by GF density
recipes.command: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli trip compare richmond charlottesville norfolk --json --select dedicated_count,avg_rating,highest_rated
recipes.explanation: After 'cities sync' for each, this asks which has the most dedicated places and the highest average rating; --select narrows the per-city JSON to the comparison fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Find safe lunch within walking distance of your hotel
recipes.explanation: Haversine over the local cache returns dedicated-GF places within 1 km of the hotel lat/lng; useful when an agent already has coordinates.

recipes.title: Surface fresh safety reports before a trip
recipes.explanation: City-wide low-rated reviews sorted newest-first; --select keeps the response under a few KB.

recipes.title: Watch your personal allowlist for safety drift
recipes.explanation: After running 'watch add <biz_id>' for places you trust, watch report re-fetches each (rate-limited) and surfaces rating drift or new low-rated reviews.

recipes.title: Compare a cuisine across trip stops
recipes.command: find-me-gluten-free-pp-cli cuisines compare burgers us va richmond charlottesville --json
recipes.explanation: The 214-cuisine taxonomy joined across cities; cuisine slug first, then country and state, then 2+ city slugs.

trigger_phrases: what changed in <city> since I last checked
